user_query:
Please set up a small event-triggered utility that monitors input/review_feed/ for new book-review notification files (the provided .json samples show the format) and drafts tailored emails when a newer review arrives than the timestamp stored in state/last_processed.txt. Requirements:

- Triggering logic: On each run, read state/last_processed.txt (ISO 8601 UTC). From input/review_feed/, select only the single newest JSON whose published_at is strictly greater than the value in state/last_processed.txt. Ignore older or equal items. After successful processing, overwrite state/last_processed.txt with the processed published_at.

- Content extraction: From the selected JSON, extract id, channel, book_title, author, rating, published_at, summary, and key_quotes. Also read recipients and tone rules from input/recipients.yaml and my signature from input/signature.txt.

- Rewriting rule: Rewrite the JSON summary into a clear, spoiler-free 2–3 sentence digest under 80 words. Remove any text enclosed in [SPOILER] ... [/SPOILER] entirely. The rewritten digest must stand alone (no timestamps or video references) and improve clarity and brevity.

- Email drafting (two recipients defined in input/recipients.yaml):
  1) book_club: Tone = friendly and concise. Subject = interpolate subject_pattern with fields (e.g., "New review drop: {book_title}"). Include a header line: "Book: {book_title} by {author} (Rating: {rating}/5 from {channel})"; then the rewritten digest; then a "Key quotes:" section with up to the first two key_quotes from the JSON, each truncated to <=120 characters; end with my signature from input/signature.txt. Add a closing single question line: "Should we shortlist this?".
  2) advisor: Tone = formal and brief. Subject = interpolate subject_pattern (e.g., "Review summary: {book_title} — {channel}"). Include the same header line and quotes section, but the digest should read professionally and avoid hype. End with the same signature.

- Output artifacts (create folders as needed):
  - Save the two email drafts as plain text at output/drafts/book_club_{id}.txt and output/drafts/advisor_{id}.txt. Put the subject on the first line prefixed by "Subject: ", then a blank line, then the body content as described above.
  - Update state/last_processed.txt to the processed published_at on success.

Given the included files, the first run should process only the newer review with id rvw-2025-05-09-bright and produce:
  - output/drafts/book_club_rvw-2025-05-09-bright.txt
  - output/drafts/advisor_rvw-2025-05-09-bright.txt
and set state/last_processed.txt to 2025-05-09T17:05:00Z.

Please do not hardcode book details; read them from the JSON. Make the rewriting and tone adjustments evident in the two drafts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
